message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_22-55-29_claim-office-lite-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-portkey-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "customer with exactly 2 years receives 20% loyalty discount on policy base premium"
Expected behavior: When customer.yearsWithMHPCO >= 2, subtract 20% of policy base premium (sum of item bases after curse/ench surcharges) from the policy total before first-ins/fee.

IMPORTANT SCOPE NOTE per spec: "policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up contract) apply to the policy base premium (the sum of all item base premiums)". But "policy base premium" here means the sum of item bases. Re-reading spec: integration example with cursed sword shows loyalty (-20) was computed as 20% of 100 (sword base). So loyalty applies to sum of bare item bases (NOT including item-level curse/ench surcharges).

Wait — look at the spec's long-standing-customer example: 100 base + 50 curse + 30 high-ench − 20 loyalty + 10 first-ins − 15 follow-up + 5 fee = 160. The −20 loyalty is 20% of 100 (sword bare base). The +10 first-ins is 10% of 100. The −15 follow-up is 15% of 100. So loyalty/first-ins/follow-up percentages all apply to the bare item base premium (no item surcharges).

For this single-item test (sword, no curse/ench), 100 base, 20% loyalty = 20. Premium = 100 − 20 + 10 + 5 = 95.

Current error: Expected 95, received 115
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_22-55-29_claim-office-lite-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-portkey-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion. Add loyalty discount: when yearsWithMHPCO >= 2, subtract 20% of sum-of-bare-bases. Keep the architecture extensible for first-ins (already 10% of bare base) and follow-up (15% of bare base, future). The simplest may be: separate `policyBaseBare` (sum of BASE_PREMIUMS lookups, accounting for block-of-3) from `itemSurcharges` (curse+ench). Premium = policyBaseBare + itemSurcharges − loyalty(% of policyBaseBare) + firstIns(% of policyBaseBare) − followUp(% of policyBaseBare) + fee. Verify all previous tests still pass — particularly the cursed-sword (165), cursed+ench-5 (203), etc.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Wait - I changed `firstInsuranceSurcharge` to use `bareBasePremium` instead of `basePremium`. Need to verify previous tests still pass. For all previous tests, customer.yearsWithMHPCO is 0, so loyalty is 0. And for tests without curse/ench, bareBasePremium === basePremium. But for the cursed-sword test (170 G): basePremium = 150, bare = 100. Before: 150 + 15 + 5 = 170. With my change: 150 + 10 + 5 = 165. That would break it.

Per the spec note in the prompt: "the +10 first-ins is 10% of 100" — so first-ins SHOULD be 10% of bare base. But the existing cursed-sword test expected 170 (15 first-ins). Let me re-check.

Test line 154: `expect(results[0].premium).toBe(170);` for cursed sword. 100+50=150 base, +15 first-ins (10% of 150) +5 = 170. So this test asserts first-ins is on basePremium (with surcharges), NOT bare base.

But the spec example in prompt says first-ins is 10% of bare. There's a conflict. The instruction says "Keep the architecture extensible for first-ins (already 10% of bare base)". But the existing passing test expects first-ins on basePremium-with-surcharges.

Let me re-read the user message more carefully. The user says "first-ins (already 10% of bare base)" but the current test expects 170 = 150 + 15 (15 = 10% of 150). So the existing impl is 10% of basePremium-with-surcharges. The user is wrong about it being "already" 10% of bare base.

I must follow the test. Don't break passing tests. Revert first-ins to use basePremium (with surcharges):

content.text:
All 16 tests pass (15 prior + 1 new loyalty test). 

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added `LOYALTY_DISCOUNT_RATE` (0.2) and `LOYALTY_THRESHOLD_YEARS` (2) constants, added a `calculateBareBasePremium` helper that mirrors `calculateBasePremium` but uses only bare item bases (skipping curse/ench surcharges) while still honoring the block-of-3 component rule, threaded `customer` through `runScenario`, and subtracted `bareBasePremium * 0.2` from the premium when `yearsWithMHPCO >= 2`.
- Result: All 16 active tests pass (22 still `it.todo`).
- Approach: Minimal — only the loyalty branch was added. First-insurance surcharge still uses `basePremium` (with surcharges) because the existing cursed-sword test (170 G = 150 + 15 + 5) requires it. The new `calculateBareBasePremium` helper keeps the architecture extensible for follow-up contract discount (also % of bare base) without changing first-ins behavior or breaking any passing tests.
